name: jobqueue
description: In-memory job queue with a fixed-size worker pool built on stdlib sync + channels. New starts N worker goroutines; Submit enqueues a Job (blocking while the buffer is full) under a read lock; Shutdown closes the channel under an exclusive write lock so workers drain every already-accepted job and the call blocks until they all return. The RWMutex pattern guarantees no send-on-closed-channel can ever occur. Shutdown is idempotent and the whole type is safe for concurrent use.

maintain.request: Add backpressure and metrics to the queue: a non-blocking TrySubmit(job) method that enqueues immediately and returns a new ErrQueueFull error when the buffer has no free slot (instead of blocking like Submit), plus a Processed() int64 method reporting how many jobs have finished running so callers can observe throughput and confirm a drain completed.

review.bug: Submit reads q.closed and sends on the jobs channel without holding q.mu.RLock(), so a concurrent Shutdown can flip closed and close(q.jobs) mid-send, producing a data race on q.closed and a possible send-on-closed-channel panic.
